# UC-22.5.19 · MiFID II Market Abuse — Insider List Access Log Correlation Before Price-Sensitive Events

## Description

MAR requires insider lists. Correlating document or deal-room access with corporate calendar events surfaces potential leaks when access clusters immediately before sensitive milestones.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes corporate, o365 and sourcetypes deal:insider_list, deal:milestone, ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Heuristic only—human investigation required; (2) align ObjectId to deal dataroom IDs; (3) legal privilege workflow; (4) integrate insider list membership via `lookup`; (5) document SMF16 review cadence. Indexes required: `index=corporate`, `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=deal:insider_list`, `sourcetype=deal:milestone`, `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110), DLP.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (UserId, ObjectId, Operation); `index=corporate` `sourcetype="deal:insider_list"` (project_id, user_id, added_date); `index=corporate` `sourcetype="deal:milestone"` (project_id, milestone_type, milestone_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Heuristic only—human investigation required; (2) align ObjectId to deal dataroom IDs; (3) legal privilege workflow; (4) integrate insider list membership via `lookup`; (5) document SMF16 review cadence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=corporate sourcetype="deal:milestone" milestone_type="earnings_release" earliest=-180d
| eval ms=strptime(milestone_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=left project_id [
    search index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Operation="FileDownloaded" earliest=-180d
    | rename UserId as user_id
    | stats earliest(_time) as first_dl by user_id, ObjectId
  ]
| where first_dl<relative_time(ms,"-14d@d") AND first_dl>relative_time(ms,"-60d@d")
| table project_id, milestone_date, user_id, first_dl
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Market Abuse — Insider List Access Log Correlation Before Price-Sensitive Events** — MAR requires insider lists. Correlating document or deal-room access with corporate calendar events surfaces potential leaks when access clusters immediately before sensitive milestones.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (UserId, ObjectId, Operation); `index=corporate` `sourcetype="deal:insider_list"` (project_id, user_id, added_date); `index=corporate` `sourcetype="deal:milestone"` (project_id, milestone_type, milestone_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110), DLP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: corporate; **sourcetype**: deal:milestone. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=corporate, sourcetype="deal:milestone", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ms** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where first_dl<relative_time(ms,"-14d@d") AND first_dl>relative_time(ms,"-60d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: first_dl, milestone_date, ms, project_id, user_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the DLP or Microsoft 365 compliance center and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console for the same window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (correlations), Timeline, Single value (count).

## SPL

```spl
index=corporate sourcetype="deal:milestone" milestone_type="earnings_release" earliest=-180d
| eval ms=strptime(milestone_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=left project_id [
    search index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Operation="FileDownloaded" earliest=-180d
    | rename UserId as user_id
    | stats earliest(_time) as first_dl by user_id, ObjectId
  ]
| where first_dl<relative_time(ms,"-14d@d") AND first_dl>relative_time(ms,"-60d@d")
| table project_id, milestone_date, user_id, first_dl
```

## Visualization

Table (correlations), Timeline, Single value (count). Key fields: `first_dl`, `milestone_date`, `ms`, `project_id`, `user_id`.
